name: Bill Evans
abstract: William John Evans (August 16, 1929 – September 15, 1980) was an American jazz pianist and composer who worked primarily as the leader of his trio. His extensive use of impressionist harmony, block chords, innovative chord voicings, and trademark rhythmically independent "singing" melodic lines continue to influence jazz pianists today.

about_quotes.speaker: Orrin Keepnews
about_quotes.evidence: I first heard Bill Evans... eventually was able to persuade Bill that he should go in to the studio and make a record... We went in and did a basically trio record
about_quotes.doc_title: Orrin Keepnews interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Hall
about_quotes.evidence: Bill came in one night and he said, “Do you want to do a duet record and…?” ... That was great. That was really a pleasure working with Bill.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Weber
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: ...I don’t think he has the innate ability to be the next Bill Evans or that kind of thing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Weber interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, June 2, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Bruce Johnstone

about_quotes.doc_title: Bruce Johnstone interviewed by Monk Rowe, Dunkirk, New York, October 11, 2013

about_quotes.speaker: John Pizzarelli, Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: That’s what I used to do a lot. I used to think I was having fun dropping beats. That was — my Bill Evans friends used to make me screw the time up all the time...
